company_type: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production

regions: Europe, Middle East and North Africa

segments: Europe, Middle East and North Africa

dependencies: Trans-Alaska Pipeline System (TAPS) for North Slope production transport
Reliance on third-party gathering, processing, compression, transportation and pipeline facilities
Dependence on availability of diluents for crude oil and bitumen transport
Joint venture partners for operations where ConocoPhillips is not operator or lacks majority control
risks: Unavailability of adequate gathering, processing, compression, transportation, and pipeline facilities
Capacity constraints in facilities and equipment may not be sufficient for production
Construction and permitting delays for new facilities
Temporary unavailability due to market conditions, extreme weather events, permitting delays, mechanical reasons
Increased costs or curtailed production if facilities become unavailable
Failures by suppliers, contract manufacturers, contractors, joint venture partners to meet obligations

regulatory_risks: U.S. Federal Clean Air Act governing air emissions
U.S. Federal Clean Water Act governing discharges to water bodies
EU Regulation for Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH)
U.S. Federal Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act (CERCLA/Superfund)
U.S. Federal Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA)
U.S. Federal Oil Pollution Act of 1990 (OPA90)
U.S. Federal Emergency Planning and Community Right-to-Know Act (EPCRA)
U.S. Federal Safe Drinking Water Act
U.S. Department of the Interior regulations for offshore operations
EU Trading Directive and EU Emissions Trading Scheme (EU ETS)
U.K. Emissions Trading Scheme (U.K. ETS)
Alberta Technology Innovation and Emissions Reduction (TIER) regulation
British Columbia Output Based Pricing System (BC OBPS)
EU regulation on reduction of methane emissions in energy sector
Australia Safeguard Mechanism under National Greenhouse and Energy Reporting Act 2007
U.S. EPA New Source Performance Standards (OOOOb) and Emissions Guidelines (OOOOc)
U.S. EPA Methane Super Emitter Program
U.S. EPA Waste Emissions Charge (WEC) under Methane Emission Reduction Program (MERP)
White House Council on Environmental Quality NEPA Phase 2 implementation regulations
Climate Superfund laws (New York and Vermont legislation holding energy companies financially responsible for climate mitigation)
Climate Private Action laws (California, New Hampshire, Oregon bills creating private right of action for climate damages)
Hydraulic fracturing regulations at local, state, federal and national levels
Restrictions or prohibitions on hydraulic fracturing in some jurisdictions
Price controls and government-imposed limitations on production or exports
Restrictions on LNG exports (U.S. temporary pause on new LNG export authorizations)
Permitting requirements and delays for exploration, drilling, production activities
Restrictions on operations in certain areas (offshore, arctic, oil sands, unconventional plays)
Dismantlement, abandonment and restoration obligations at end of asset life
Carbon taxes in various jurisdictions (Norway, Canada)
GHG emissions trading schemes and reduction policies
Paris Agreement commitments and COP28 global stocktake recommendations
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (15% minimum tax on book income, 1% excise tax on stock repurchases)
Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (EU)
International Sustainability Standards Board reporting standards
California climate-related disclosure laws
Australian climate-related disclosure legislation
Ballot initiatives and challenges to project permits restricting oil and gas development

sanction_risks: U.S. government authority to prevent or restrict business in foreign jurisdictions
Sanctions limiting ability to operate in or access opportunities in various jurisdictions
Venezuela sanctions impacting collection of arbitration awards
Compliance with U.S. sanctions against Venezuela in arbitration award collection actions
Diplomatic relations between U.S. and foreign jurisdictions affecting ability to collect awards
Restrictions on commodity exports and imports in response to domestic energy costs or national emergency
OPEC and other producing countries' actions affecting supply and prices
